checklist.note: Implements the F9 design exactly: core/sync_check.py relocated to engineering/sync_check.py (git mv, 100% similarity — byte-identical), cli/sync.py imports the new path, super_harness.engineering added to the core-is-base forbidden set, and d-core-is-base re-ratified (all three body prose spots + core/__init__ docstring + adapters/__init__ comment now name the four upper layers cli/gates/sensors/engineering).

Only the declared 10-file scope touched; out_of_scope empty. The one file added over v1/v2 (adapters/__init__.py) folds in the code-review NIT rather than shipping self-inconsistent architecture prose. d-core-is-base amended via re-ratify (tier-1, has a check) — not a tier-2 reconcile; its five @decision anchors are unaffected (the code anchor core/__init__.py stays).

Two-actor review to convergence. Plan review r1 (Codex + Claude subagent) REVISE — both flagged core/__init__.py missing from scope + the decision body naming the forbidden set in 3 places not 1 → folded into v2. Code review (Codex + Claude subagent) APPROVE; Codex NIT (stale adapters comment) → folded into v3. v3 delta re-confirmed APPROVE by both actors. Subagent independently recomputed compute_body_hash(body) == re-stamped ratified_text_hash (MATCH).

Behaviourally inert relocation (run_sync_check unchanged; no core-relative assumptions at the new home; no self-import). No dangling super_harness. core.sync_check references in live code/tests (remaining hits are historical plan docs + the decision-body #56 history/F9 note, which self-neutralizes). Contract renamed by name only; every consumer pins the id core-is-base, not the name string.

Faithfulness surfaces all updated and mutually consistent: .importlinter contract name, d-core-is-base body (headline + list + stack phrase + F9 parenthetical), core/__init__ docstring, adapters/__init__ TYPE_CHECKING comment. sync --check clean; AGENTS.md / cli-reference have zero sync_check references (nothing to regenerate).

verdict.summary: Relocate sync_check out of core and extend the core-is-base architecture-fitness contract to engineering — closing a direct core->engineering import edge the contract had never listed (a real blind spot in the project's own G-FITNESS dimension). Pure relocation + contract extension + re-ratify; 1644 tests pass; ruff / mypy / lint-imports (core-is-base KEPT on the 4-layer forbidden set) / decision check / sync --check all clean. Two-actor plan+code review converged to APPROVE across v1->v2->v3 fold-ins.
